assumptions: Range decreases linearly at the configured closure velocity.
Bus kills remove every carried torplet; release changes inventory form but not torplet-equivalent count.
All weapon layers share the live inventory, so a target can be credited to only one weapon.
Template target, jammer, and chaff positions retain their initial relative geometry as range changes.
Gross laser service capacity is reduced by the explicit association-efficiency schedule and by thermal availability.

scenario_name: TF Sahara first SICKLE salvo — severe contested baseline
warnings: Laser checkpoints call the constant-range Lidar/PD model at each interval midpoint; closure within that interval is not sub-stepped.
Association efficiency and interceptor conditional kill probabilities are scenario inputs, not predictions produced by Lidar/PD.
Expected fractional capacities are accumulated deterministically and credited as whole kills without Monte Carlo randomness.
